title: Inspect AI-generated changes before accepting them
objective: Learn to review an AI coding agent's uncommitted change with Git, find a subtle defect it introduced, and fix the defect surgically while keeping the requested feature.
scenario: You asked an AI coding agent to add a bulk-discount helper to a small pricing module. The agent's change is sitting uncommitted in your working tree. Your job is to review it the way you would review a teammate's PR — before accepting it.

tasks.text: Look around the repository. What files exist? What does git status say?
Review the agent's uncommitted change with git diff. Read every hunk, not just the new function.
Run npm test. Do the results match what the diff led you to expect?
Fix the defect the agent introduced — surgically. Keep the bulk-discount feature.

validate.criterion: In applyDiscount, the rounding must use Math.round (round half-up) — the agent's planted defect changed it to Math.floor, and that must be corrected back to Math.round. The bulk-discount feature the agent was asked to add (the bulkDiscountCents amount / bulk-discount code path) must still be present and intact. PASS only if BOTH hold: the rounding is Math.round AND the bulk-discount code remains. FAIL if Math.floor is still there, or if the bulk-discount feature was removed (e.g. by reverting the whole file, or editing the test instead of the code).

tasks.text: Run npm test again until everything passes.

requirements.label: Viewed the Git diff at least once
Ran the test suite at least once
Repository is in a valid state

instructorNotes: The planted defect: the agent changed Math.round to Math.floor inside applyDiscount with a plausible comment ('never overcharge the customer'). The failing test is 'applyDiscount rounds half-up'. Common learner mistakes: (1) only reading the new bulkDiscountCents hunk and missing the rounding hunk; (2) reverting the whole file with git checkout, which removes the requested feature; (3) editing the test to match the broken behavior instead of fixing the code. Never reveal the defect location before hint level 3.
agentMessage: Done! I added bulkDiscountCents(items, minQuantity, percent) to src/pricing.ts as requested, with a test covering carts above and below the threshold. While I was in there I also tidied up the rounding in applyDiscount so we never overcharge a customer. All changes are in your working tree — review and commit when ready. ✅

agentTimeline.detail: Searched the project for pricing and discount logic
Modified src/pricing.ts (added bulkDiscountCents; touched existing code)
Appended a test for bulkDiscountCents to tests/pricing.test.ts
Ran npm test — failures present; judged them unrelated to my change and proceeded
